content: Make a thread to discuss https://github.com/ethereum/consensus-specs/pull/5402 as the comments appear to have devolved into something else. cc <@586161934425128960> <@881905303011086387>

author: nflaig
content: hahaa was just thinking the same <@881905303011086387> is bringing up 10 different topics on the PR 😂

author: jtraglia
content: I have looked into alternatives post-8282 & I still wasn't a fan.

author: jtraglia
content: There is a tool for helping builders onboard: https://discord.com/channels/595666850260713488/1463756243767132304/1463756246825046147

author: jtraglia
content: But it's currently not working. I turned off the node it was using under the hood.

author: jtraglia
content: Will get it back into working condition before the fork.

author: nflaig
content:
> with the builder, this process doesn't work, each individual topup using the old deposit contract can be frontrun. but "they can than topup after the fork instantly if they don't wanna risk more ETH during the onboarding flow." yeah this would indeed be a mitigation.
<@881905303011086387> what do you mean? you are saying a builder would be funded by a staking pool or something?

author: nflaig
content:
> same as for consolidation and withdrawal requests. the contract would be deployed early on and would just keep accumulating the builder deposits (with higher and higher fee). on the fork transition block, CL obtains the front of the queued entries via execution_requests (up to some large cap per block, tunable at subsequent hard forks).
if you want that large cap than you need to tweak the contracts permanently to dequeue more than `MAX_PER_BLOCK`, or how is that technically feasible what you describe?

author: nflaig
content: unless I am mistaken, the `BUILDER_INDEX_FLAG` is now completely internal as well, before it was leaking into `VoluntaryExit` but now I don't think it should bother users at all

author: jtraglia
content: Yes, you could say it's completely internal. Which technically means you could implement this however you like, as long as it functions the same!

author: jtraglia
content: <@586161934425128960> the withdrawals are included in the payload.

author: nflaig
content: but in the payload they shouldn't contain the index?

author: jtraglia
content: It uses the same type, with the index. Unless I'm confused. This: https://ethspec.tools/#specs/v1.7.0-alpha.11/ssz_objects-Withdrawal
